mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 9.0% signals labor market deterioration that constrains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 9.0%, or cumulative forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move in tandem above their 12-month trend, jointly signaling robust consumer demand and residential investment that sustains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages simultaneously
failure_condition: Interaction signal (both RSAFS and HOUST above trend) produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness that reduces corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO declines > 0.5% produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 net new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains elevated above 8.5%, jointly signaling a stalled labor market recovery that constrains aggregate demand and equity earnings, producing negative 60-day returns.
failure_condition: Joint condition (weak payroll growth and high unemployment) produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling monetary policy lag that erodes real returns and forces future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as rate expectations shift.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: Policy lag condition produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) decline below 500,000 units annualized, jointly indicating demand-side stagnation that suppresses corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 500,000 units annualized
failure_condition: Joint demand weakness condition produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows steadily, jointly signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor demand that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds 6-month moving average AND PAYEMS monthly change > 100,000
failure_condition: Joint supply-labor strength condition produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 57% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.
